contextBanner: [TBG | Agentic Operations Adoption | coordinator/toolchain | branch: docs/agent-skills-stale-pr-cherry-pick]
purpose: Expose BlacksmithGuild's repo-specific contracts, skills, compact evidence, and proof boundaries to external agent-operation tools without vendoring those tools or confusing coordination evidence with runtime proof.

toolClassifications.repoImpact: reads repo rules, skills, workflows, compact packets, PR state, and dispatches bounded work
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: one coordinator consumes compact crewmate reports instead of full terminal transcripts
toolClassifications.risk: coordinator policy can drift from repo authority or dispatch into overlapping lanes

toolClassifications.repoImpact: leases reusable isolated worktrees while retaining dependencies and build caches
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: stable lane identity and cached workspaces reduce repeated setup and branch archaeology
toolClassifications.risk: a lease can outlive its PR or conceal evidence-retention obligations

toolClassifications.repoImpact: can gate docs, skills, validators, static contracts, lint, tests, push, PR creation, and CI
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: returns a bounded gate verdict instead of asking an agent to narrate every check
toolClassifications.risk: generic disposable-worktree gates cannot certify Bannerlord process, save, loaded-DLL, or gameplay state without a TBG adapter

toolClassifications.repoImpact: can iterate on static docs, classification, schemas, fixtures, and validators with commit/rollback boundaries
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: committed iterations and exit summaries replace chat-by-chat supervision
toolClassifications.risk: unsafe for live gameplay, launcher, command-inbox, or save mutation without explicit bounded stop conditions

toolClassifications.repoImpact: shapes a future Invoke-TbgAxi.ps1 while retaining ForgeAgentStatus.cmd as the compatibility wrapper
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: minimal defaults, aggregates, truncation, structured errors, exit codes, and contextual next commands
toolClassifications.risk: an overly terse interface can hide proof level, freshness, or blocker detail

toolClassifications.repoImpact: reviews generated sprint maps, PR topology, proof ladders, worker graphs, and operator guides
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: annotations target visual nodes or sections instead of repasting an entire plan
toolClassifications.risk: visual approval is not build, contract, or runtime proof

toolClassifications.repoImpact: provides a future distribution path for explicitly public TBG skills
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: agents load one reusable skill package rather than long copied prompts
toolClassifications.risk: repo-internal skills may expose local paths or stale policy if published without packaging review

toolClassifications.repoImpact: hosts named sessions and terminal-agnostic TBG commands
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: durable panes keep commands and compact outputs visible outside chat
toolClassifications.risk: personal configuration or terminal layout can be mistaken for repo truth

toolClassifications.repoImpact: backs persistent coordinator sessions where supported
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: agents resume named sessions rather than reconstructing shell state
toolClassifications.risk: native Windows availability must not be assumed

toolClassifications.repoImpact: optional keyboard-first editor and review surface
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: keeps focused edits and diagnostics in the persistent terminal workspace
toolClassifications.risk: must not become a required repo dependency

toolClassifications.repoImpact: feeds structured sprint requests and packet annotations
toolClassifications.tokenSavingMechanism: dictation accelerates intent capture while the relay carries machine state
toolClassifications.risk: private dictated data, transcription errors, and OS-specific assumptions

repoProvides: static validators and exact runtime commands
neverInfer: runtime PASS from a PR being mergeable
loaded DLL identity from a built file alone
named save identity from native Continue
movement, arrival, trade delta, or visible UI from command ACK
permission for destructive cleanup from general write authority

treehouseLeaseModel.retention: retain until proof artifacts are archived and PR disposition is recorded

treehouseLeaseModel.purpose: AGENTS, CLAUDE, skills, contracts, and compendium work
treehouseLeaseModel.retention: release after merged head and clean status are verified

treehouseLeaseModel.purpose: ForgeAgentStatus and future AXI packet work
treehouseLeaseModel.retention: release after wrapper/script checks and merged PR

treehouseLeaseModel.purpose: selective replay from one classified stale stack
treehouseLeaseModel.retention: release only after provenance and old-PR disposition are recorded
protect until a verified archive manifest and restore path exist

outputRules: full detail only behind an explicit full switch
structured errors and stable exit codes
freshness and proof level on every evidence verdict
no raw huge JSON by default

gnhfForbiddenWithoutRuntimeContract: tasks whose only success signal is human observation

skillDistribution.packageRule: A public installable skill must have standard SKILL.md frontmatter, no machine-local paths or secrets, explicit authority boundaries, and a versioned source contract. Repo-internal skills remain clearly marked and are not published by implication.

orderedSteps.name: merge_doctrine_and_compendium
exitEvidence: skill registry and workflow JSON parse
agentic operations contract and skill are committed

orderedSteps.name: close_route_proof_gap
exitEvidence: PR #43 exact head built and loaded identity match
unattended route start and numeric movement evidence
Manual or hold cleanup state verified

orderedSteps.name: ship_tbg_axi_surface
exitEvidence: status, prs, worktrees, packet, proof, and next commands
bounded output and stable exit codes

orderedSteps.name: adopt_external_coordinator_in_inspection_mode
exitEvidence: Firstmate and Treehouse verified outside the repo
one read-only or docs-only lease consumes TBG contracts and packet

orderedSteps.name: replay_and_close_one_stale_stack
exitEvidence: old PR close or retention rationale

requires: external tools and repo harness are kept as separate cooperating layers
every tool has category, install scope, repo impact, token-saving mechanism, risk, and priority
treehouse lease and evidence-retention boundaries are explicit
AXI commands and output rules are explicit
no-mistakes and gnhf runtime boundaries are explicit
skill distribution distinguishes repo-internal from public-installable packages
the most rewarding sprint order is recorded
git diff --check passes or the exact environment blocker is recorded
